title: Comments
subtitle: Notes for humans
goal: Learn what comments are and why programmers use them.
explanation: Comments are explanations inside the code.
JavaScript completely ignores comments when running the program.
They are for people—to explain tricky parts or leave notes.
Good comments explain why something was done, not just repeat the code.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: A comment is like a sticky note. A person reads it, but JavaScript walks right past it.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: You don't need to comment every line of code. Comments should explain what isn't obvious.
